div1.entryFree: Cover, subst. any thing laid over another to hide or screen or contain it: the c. of the salt hides the salt, Gentl. III, 1, 369. they (the news) have a good c.; they show well outward, Ado I, 2, 8. death is the fairest c. for her shame, IV, 1, 117. my form . . . is yet the c. of a fairer mind, John IV, 2, 258. that small model of the barren earth which serves as paste and c. to our bones, R2 III, 2, 154. this unbound lover only lacks a c. (like a book) Rom. I, 3, 88. the c. (of the carriage) of the wings of grasshoppers, I, 4, 60.
